evals.prompt: Generate a README for our NestJS + React + PostgreSQL project management app. It has user auth, projects, tasks, team collaboration, and file attachments. Deployed on AWS ECS.
evals.expected_output: Complete README with project description, tech stack, setup instructions, API overview, and deployment

assertions.name: project_description
assertions.description: Clear description of what the app does

assertions.name: tech_stack
assertions.description: NestJS, React, PostgreSQL, AWS listed with versions

assertions.name: setup_instructions
assertions.description: Step-by-step local development setup

assertions.name: api_overview
assertions.description: Key API endpoints listed

assertions.name: deployment
assertions.description: AWS ECS deployment instructions or reference

evals.prompt: Create a README for an open-source CLI tool written in Go. It converts CSV files to JSON. Support for: piping stdin, file arguments, custom delimiters, and output formatting.
evals.expected_output: README for CLI tool with installation, usage examples, flags, and piping examples

assertions.name: installation
assertions.description: go install or binary download instructions

assertions.name: usage_examples
assertions.description: Multiple usage examples with different flags

assertions.name: stdin_piping
assertions.description: Example showing cat file.csv | tool

assertions.name: flag_reference
assertions.description: All flags documented with descriptions

assertions.name: contributing
assertions.description: Contributing guide section

evals.prompt: Generate a README for a Python library published on PyPI. The library provides data validation utilities. Include: installation via pip, quick start, API reference for 3 main functions, and comparison with alternatives (Pydantic, marshmallow).
evals.expected_output: Library README with pip install, quick start code, API docs, and alternatives comparison

assertions.name: pip_install
assertions.description: pip install command prominently displayed

assertions.name: quick_start
assertions.description: Minimal working code example

assertions.name: api_reference
assertions.description: Documentation for main functions with parameters and return types

assertions.name: alternatives
assertions.description: Comparison with Pydantic and marshmallow

assertions.name: badges
assertions.description: PyPI version, license, and CI badges

evals.prompt: Update our README — the project added 3 new features (webhooks, CSV export, dark mode) and switched from Jest to Vitest for testing. The existing README is outdated.
evals.expected_output: Updated README with new features added, test command updated, no other sections broken

assertions.name: features_added
assertions.description: Webhooks, CSV export, and dark mode mentioned in features

assertions.name: test_command_updated
assertions.description: Test section uses vitest instead of jest

assertions.name: existing_preserved
assertions.description: Existing sections not removed or broken

assertions.name: incremental
assertions.description: Only changed sections are modified

assertions.name: consistent_style
assertions.description: New content matches existing README style

evals.prompt: Create a README for a Docker-based development toolkit. It provides a docker-compose setup with PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO (S3-compatible), and Mailhog (email testing). One command to start everything.
evals.expected_output: README focused on quick start, service descriptions, port mappings, and access URLs

assertions.name: quick_start
assertions.description: Single docker-compose up command prominently shown

assertions.name: service_list
assertions.description: All 4 services described with their purpose

assertions.name: port_mappings
assertions.description: Port mappings for each service clearly documented

assertions.name: access_urls
assertions.description: URLs for accessing each service (MinIO console, Mailhog web UI)

assertions.name: env_config
assertions.description: Environment variable customization documented
